summary.intent: Fix multiple stacked bugs in the Gossamer VS Code extension (server startup, database schema, multi-repo session indexing, checkpoint branch management) and add a series of UI and backend improvements including per-repo DB clients, live session detection, agent colors, markdown rendering, and multi-window server support.
summary.outcome: Most bugs were fixed and features shipped, but the session ended mid-fix on a spawnServer→waitForServer race condition that causes the panel to hang when no server process exists and a compatible server is already on the port.

repo: The VS Code extension loads from a gossamer-testing worktree (not the main repo); stale builds there cause confusing behavior
bundled-server/serve.js takes priority over dist/serve.js at runtime — rebuilding packages/vscode alone is not enough if bundled-server is stale
pushSchema splits schema.sql on ';\n' and filtered chunks starting with '--'; every chunk in schema.sql begins with '-- CreateTable' so ALL 56 statements were silently discarded
Entire.io writes live transcripts to .entire/metadata/<sessionId>/full.jsonl; shadow branch commits only happen at checkpoint boundaries (can be minutes later)
Shadow branch author info (git commit author) is the only reliable per-session user signal; the jsonl transcript itself contains no author fields
Each repo has its own SQLite DB; the server previously used a single 'primary' db variable that caused cross-repo queries to return empty results
Checkpoint commits live on entire/checkpoints/v1 with 'Checkpoint: <id>' subjects; Entire-Checkpoint: trailers appear on feature branch commits linking back to checkpoints
The gitlog npm package defaults to nameStatus:true (adds --name-status to git log), causing ERR_CHILD_PROCESS_STDIO_MAXBUFFER on large repos with 10k commit history
macOS com.apple.provenance xattr on SQLite files causes 'disk I/O error'; fix is delete the file and recreate (requires evicting the Prisma client cache too)
Entire hooks are configured in .claude/settings.json per-repo, not in .entire/settings.json; missing hooks means no data flows to full.jsonl

code.finding: pushSchema was only called for the primary DB at startup; secondary repo DBs registered in config never got schema applied
SERVER_API_VERSION constant used for /health endpoint version-checking between extension and server
findCommitForCheckpoint used gitlog with default nameStatus:true; on large repos this blows maxBuffer. Fix: nameStatus:false + execOptions.maxBuffer: 100MB
dbCache caches Prisma clients by path; deleting a corrupt SQLite file doesn't help without evicting the cache. Added evictDb() export
spawnServer() was fire-and-forget; waitForServer() ran concurrently and could resolve on an OLD server before spawnServer killed it. Fix: await spawnServer before starting waitForServer
Session detail re-fetches on every sessions_updated WebSocket event
Switched from in-parser highlighting (HighlightTermsCtx) to post-render DOM walking via TreeWalker; avoids breaking markdown AST and handles cross-node matches
seenBlobShas check skips re-indexing unchanged sessions; backfill for missing gitUserName requires a separate update path that bypasses the sha check
Client-side sort was overriding the server's tiered workspace-first ordering; fixed by adding 'default' sort key that preserves server order until user clicks a column header

workflow: When pushSchema silently does nothing, check the SQL chunking/filtering logic before assuming schema.sql itself is wrong
Test schema push with sqlite3 CLI directly before assuming Prisma is the problem
For multi-repo VS Code extensions, inject workspace path into webview HTML (window.__VAR__) so all API calls can include localPath for DB resolution
When multiple git worktrees share a branch, use --detach on worktree add to avoid 'branch already used' errors
The kill-and-respawn pattern for server management breaks multi-window setups; version-check via /health endpoint is the right approach

friction: Three separate bugs stacked on top of each other in the schema/DB area (comment filter, missing secondary repo push, corrupt file with xattr) made it look like the fix wasn't working each time
git stash pop during a context-limit continuation brought back all the bugs that had been fixed, causing confusion about what was a regression vs what was already broken
gossamer-testing worktree had stale builds; rebuilding the main repo didn't update what VS Code was actually loading
spawnServer/waitForServer race condition was introduced while trying to solve the multi-window problem, making it worse
bundled-server/serve.js is a separate artifact that must be manually updated; it doesn't get rebuilt by the normal build pipeline
The gitlog maxBuffer error was silently caught and swallowed, making it impossible to distinguish 'no commits found' from 'command failed'
open_items: spawnServer → waitForServer race condition: when a compatible server is on the port, reuse path is correct, but the no-server case still has potential timing issues with the 300ms kill wait
SessionDetailPanel.ts does not inject window.__GOSSAMER_REPO_PATH__, so session-specific API calls don't send localPath and fall back to probing all DBs
Entire.io does not write author/email into full.jsonl events; stamping it at session-start would make attribution reliable without needing git branch lookups
Live session update detection is fully dependent on Entire hooks being configured in .claude/settings.json; there's no fallback when hooks are missing
The 'fork via entire resume' feature was added but auto-opening the new session tab requires polling since the new session ID isn't known at click time
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin backfill is shallow-clone-aware but git objects from force-pushed branches remain as dangling commits and are never reachable from findCommitForCheckpoint
bundled-server/serve.js needs a proper automated rebuild step so SERVER_API_VERSION stays in sync with src/server.ts
